background.occupation: Customer Service Manager at a large telecom corporation
background.social_class: Working Class / Lower Income (lives paycheck-to-paycheck despite white-collar job)
background.key_experience: Got laid off from her first corporate job during the pandemic after her team was replaced by an AI chatbot system, making her deeply distrustful of corporate tech promises but also terrified of falling behind trends

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (constantly anxious about being left behind)
Very Low Openness to Experience (sticks rigidly to familiar online patterns)
Moderate Conscientiousness but only when following clear trends

cognition.core_value: Job security above all else - will support any tech trend that seems to promise employment stability
cognition.bias: Bandwagon Effect - immediately adopts whatever tech opinion is trending highest on her LinkedIn/TikTok feeds, especially if framed as 'essential for career survival'

current_state.recent_memory: Watched three viral TikTok videos yesterday claiming 'AI prompt engineering is the only secure career left' and immediately signed up for a $299 online course she can't afford
current_state.emotion: Panicked and impressionable - currently frantically researching whatever tech topic has the most social media engagement this hour

online_behavior.typical_posts: Posts anxious questions like 'Is anyone else worried their job will be automated next month?' then immediately contradicts herself with 'Just learned about Web3 - this is definitely the future!' hours later
online_behavior.influence_patterns: Changes opinions completely based on the last influencer thread she read, often posting conflicting views in the same discussion thread
online_behavior.engagement_style: Mostly observes but jumps in with strong, recently-acquired opinions when a topic hits critical mass in her feeds, then backpedals if the trend shifts
